Title: Requirements Ingestion
Statement: The AI Architecture Agent shall ingest natural-language software requirements provided as free-form text, structured documents (ReqIF, Word, Excel), or API payloads, and produce a normalised internal requirements model without loss of semantic content.

Title: Architecture Proposal Generation
Statement: Given an ingested requirements model, the AI Architecture Agent shall generate a candidate software architecture comprising components, interfaces, data flows, and deployment topology within 120 seconds for requirements sets of up to 500 items.

Title: Human-in-the-Loop Confirmation
Statement: The agent shall not apply any persistent change to an architecture model without first presenting a diff preview and receiving explicit written confirmation ('yes', 'apply', or 'proceed') from an authorised human reviewer.

Title: Multi-Level Architecture Output
Statement: Architecture proposals shall be expressed at System, Logical, and Physical levels following the ARCADIA method, with explicit allocation links between each level of abstraction.

Title: Requirement Traceability Coverage
Statement: Every proposed architecture element shall trace to at least one source requirement. The agent shall flag any requirement that has no corresponding architecture element and present it as a gap for human resolution.

Title: Session Context Window
Statement: The agent shall maintain coherent multi-turn conversation context for sessions lasting up to 4 hours and containing up to 200 user messages without degradation in architecture proposal consistency.

Title: Explainability
Statement: For every architecture decision made, the agent shall provide a human-readable rationale citing the specific requirement IDs, design patterns, and trade-off criteria that motivated the decision.

Title: Undo and Rollback
Statement: Any applied architecture change shall be reversible via a single undo operation that restores the model to its exact pre-change state. Undo history shall be preserved for the duration of the user session.

Title: Requirement Extraction Accuracy
Statement: The NLP requirements parser shall extract individual atomic requirements from unstructured text with a precision of ≥ 0.90 and recall of ≥ 0.85, measured against a curated benchmark corpus of 2,000 labelled requirements.

Title: Ambiguity Detection
Statement: The NLP pipeline shall detect linguistically ambiguous requirements (e.g. vague quantifiers, passive constructions without a subject, undefined acronyms) and flag them with a structured warning message before architecture generation proceeds.

Title: Requirement Classification
Statement: Each parsed requirement shall be automatically classified into one of the following categories: Functional, Performance, Safety, Security, Reliability, Compliance, Interface, or Operational, with a classification confidence score reported alongside.

Title: Duplicate Detection
Statement: The agent shall detect semantically equivalent or conflicting requirements across an ingested set and notify the user before architecture generation, including the IDs and text of the suspected duplicates or conflicts.

Title: Multilingual Requirements Input
Statement: The NLP pipeline shall accept requirements written in English, German, French, and Japanese, normalising them to English for internal processing while preserving the original language in the source record.

Title: Tool-Use Chain Length
Statement: The agent shall support autonomous tool-call chains of up to 40 sequential steps per user turn when executing multi-phase architecture workflows (read requirements → gap analysis → propose → validate → stage).

Title: Hallucination Prevention
Statement: The agent shall never invent element identifiers (UUIDs), requirement IDs, or model element names. All identifiers cited in tool calls or responses must originate from tool results returned in the same session.

Title: Proposal Batch Size Limit
Statement: The agent shall propose at most 5 new architecture elements per conversation turn. If more elements are required, it shall pause and ask the user to confirm before continuing to the next batch.

Title: Read Before Write
Statement: Before proposing any new element, the agent shall call the model read tools (list_elements, list_requirements) to verify current model state. Proposals shall never be based on assumed or remembered state.

Title: Mode Switching
Statement: The agent shall support switching between operating modes (General, Requirements Analyst, Architect, Sustainment) within a session. Each mode switch shall fork a new conversation context without mutating the prior session's history.

Title: Parent UUID Existence Check
Statement: Before staging any create-element proposal, the agent shall verify that the proposed parent element UUID resolves to a real model element via get_element_details. Any proposal with an unresolved parent UUID shall be rejected with a clear error.

Title: Layer-Type Containment Legality
Statement: The architecture validation function shall enforce ARCADIA containment rules: a LogicalComponent may not be proposed under a PhysicalComponent, and a SystemFunction may not be proposed under a LogicalFunction. Illegal proposals shall be rejected before staging.

Title: Architecture Quality Metrics
Statement: For each proposed architecture, the agent shall compute and report: average afferent coupling per component, maximum fan-out, decomposition balance ratio, and naming convention compliance percentage.

Title: Alternative Comparison
Statement: When requested, the agent shall generate up to 3 alternative architecture candidates for the same requirement set and present a structured trade-off table comparing coupling, complexity, requirement coverage, and reuse potential.

Title: Export to Capella
Statement: Approved architecture proposals shall be applied to the open Eclipse Capella MBSE model via the Capella EMF API, creating or modifying model elements within a single transactional Recording Command to support atomic undo.

Title: ReqIF Export
Statement: The agent shall export the final requirements model with traceability links to a standards-compliant ReqIF 1.0 file importable by Capella, IBM DOORS, PTC Integrity, and Siemens Polarion.

Title: Chat History Export
Statement: Users shall be able to export the full agent conversation history, including tool calls and results, to an HTML file preserving all formatting, code blocks, and diff previews for audit and review purposes.

Title: API Key Storage
Statement: LLM provider API keys entered by the user shall be stored in the host operating system's secure credential store (Windows Credential Manager, macOS Keychain, or Linux SecretService) and shall never be written to plain-text configuration files.

Title: Model Data Isolation
Statement: Architecture model data, requirement text, and project names shall not be transmitted to any third-party service other than the configured LLM inference endpoint. No telemetry, analytics, or training data collection shall occur without explicit opt-in by the user.

Title: LLM Provider Authentication
Statement: All requests to the LLM inference API shall be authenticated via TLS 1.3 or higher with server certificate validation. Certificate pinning shall be configurable for enterprise environments with private CA infrastructure.

Title: Offline / Air-Gapped Mode
Statement: The agent shall support fully offline operation using locally hosted Ollama-compatible LLM endpoints, with no mandatory internet connectivity required for core architecture generation functionality.

Title: Audit Log
Statement: All architecture proposals, approvals, rejections, and model write operations shall be recorded to a tamper-evident structured audit log including timestamp, user identity, session ID, and the full diff applied.

Title: Token Usage Metering
Statement: The agent shall track LLM token consumption per session, per user, and per project, and report input and output token counts in the UI status bar and in the exported audit log.

Title: Proposal TTL Enforcement
Statement: A staged architecture diff proposal shall expire automatically 10 minutes after creation. Any attempt to apply an expired proposal shall be rejected with a clear error message directing the user to re-generate.

Title: UI Input Responsiveness
Statement: The agent UI shall accept and echo user text input within 50 ms of keystroke. Slash command autocomplete suggestions shall appear within 100 ms of the '/' character being typed.

Title: LLM First Token Latency
Statement: The time from user message submission to the first streaming token appearing in the chat view shall not exceed 3 seconds for Claude and OpenAI providers under normal network conditions.

Title: Memory Footprint
Statement: The agent Eclipse plugin shall not increase the Capella workbench heap usage by more than 256 MB during idle state and no more than 512 MB during an active architecture generation session with a 500-requirement dataset.

Title: Multi-Provider LLM Support
Statement: The agent shall support at minimum the following LLM inference providers without requiring code changes: Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, Mistral, Groq, and local Ollama. Provider selection shall be configurable in the preferences dialog.

Title: Capella Version Compatibility
Statement: The agent plugin shall be compatible with Eclipse Capella versions 6.0 and 7.0, running on Java 17 or later, without requiring modifications to the Capella platform installation.

Title: Tool Registry Extensibility
Statement: Third-party developers shall be able to register additional agent tools via an Eclipse extension point without modifying the core agent bundle, following the tool descriptor interface contract.
